nameMeanings.name: Sutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Sutton, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English surname is also common in Ireland (Wexford, Kildare), where it has been established since the 13th century and Gaelicized as de Sutún.
Jewish (from the former Ottoman Empire, including Syria): unexplained.
English: topographic name from Middle English (bi) suthentune ‘(at the place to the) south of the village’.

variants.name: Setton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Ronit, Yossef.  French Andree, Henri, Jacques, Maryse.
Jewish (from the Ottoman Empire): altered form of   Sutton  .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Sutton was born about 1305, in England, United Kingdom, her father, John Sutton I, was 21 and her mother, Margaret Somery, was 14. She married Ralph Josselyn about 1319, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1354, in her hometown, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda Sutton est née environ 1305, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Sutton I, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margaret Somery, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Ralph Josselyn environ 1319, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1354, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda Sutton um 1305 in England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Sutton I 21 und ihre Mutter Margaret Somery 14. Sie hat um 1319 in Essex, England Ralph Josselyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1354 im Alter von 51 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda Sutton è nata il circa 1305 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Sutton I, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Margaret Somery, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Ralph Josselyn circa 1319, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1354, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 51 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Sutton(이)가 대략 1305년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Sutton I(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Somery(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 대략 1319년 영국 에세스에서 Ralph Josselyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1354년 그녀의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.
